description_long: This object is an ornate flintlock-style rifle or pistol, featuring a wooden stock with a rich, brown finish and a metal barrel adorned with decorative engravings. The barrel is relatively short and flares outward at the muzzle, suggesting a blunderbuss or early pistol design. The lock mechanism is prominently visible, with a classic hammer and frizzen assembly typical of flintlock firearms. The trigger guard and other metal fittings are dark, possibly iron or steel, and contrast with the lighter wood. The overall craftsmanship suggests a historical or antique weapon, likely from the 18th or early 19th century. The object is compact and would be easily carried or displayed in an urban environment, such as a museum, collector's home, or themed establishment.
description: An ornate, antique flintlock rifle or pistol with a wooden stock and engraved metal barrel, featuring a flared muzzle and classic lock mechanism.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden stock, engraved barrel, and detailed lock mechanism.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the flared muzzle and the alignment of the barrel, with the lock mechanism partially visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wood grain of the stock and the engraved barrel, with the lock mechanism seen from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the rounded end of the wooden stock and the length of the barrel extending forward.
